title: dactylorchis maculata subsp. elodes
intro: Dactylorchis maculata subsp. elodes, commonly known as the autumn lady's tresses, is a subspecies of orchid native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other compounds. The autumn lady's tresses is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although its use should be carefully considered due to potential interactions with other medications.
